subject.name: Linear App
subject.tagline: Linear is a product development platform focused on issue tracking and project management for software teams. Founded in 2019, it emphasizes speed, clean design, and developer experience, serving over 25,000 companies including OpenAI, Coinbase, and Ramp. It uses a product-led growth model with simple, transparent pricing and powerful network effects to spread organically within organizations. Linear is remote-first, profitable, and has raised $52 million in funding.
subject.subCategory: Linear delivers a best-in-class user experience for software issue tracking, prioritizing speed, simplicity, and developer experience over configurability, making it ideal for startups and mid-stage software teams.
notable: Profitable with 25 engineers
Expanded user base to over 25,000 companies
Launched AI-powered agents
Remote-first with international team

competitors.name: Jira
competitors.tagline: A comprehensive project management and issue tracking platform designed for agile development teams.
competitors.subCategory: Offers a massive marketplace ecosystem of over 3,000 apps and deep enterprise-grade customization and compliance features.
notable: Jira's Spring 2026 release introduced AI agents that understand work, a friendlier UI, and enhanced admin controls.
Atlassian is rebuilding its Cloud platform in 2025 with AI at the core, including Rovo.
Jira scaled to support up to 100,000 users in a single instance.
Atlassian launched an open beta of AI agents in Jira and invested in MCP.

competitors.name: ClickUp
competitors.tagline: An all-in-one productivity platform combining project management, docs, goals, and communication.
competitors.subCategory: Positions itself as the 'everything app' for work, targeting entire organizations rather than just software development teams.
notable: Released ClickUp 4.0 with new AI assistant features and two types of AI agents.
ClickUp Brain connects tasks, documents, people, and company knowledge.
Acquired Qatalog in November 2025 to accelerate AI convergence strategy.

competitors.name: Asana
competitors.tagline: A work management platform designed to help teams organize, track, and manage their work.
competitors.subCategory: Excels at cross-functional project management and timeline visualization for non-technical teams.
notable: Launched AI Studio and AI Teammates for customizing AI agents.
Launched Asana Gov for government and regulated industries in November 2025.
Raised $200M Convertible Note at $10.5B valuation led by Benchmark and Founders Fund.

competitors.name: monday.com
competitors.tagline: A visual work operating system for managing workflows, projects, and team collaboration.
competitors.subCategory: Uses a highly visual, colorful, and intuitive interface that is easily adopted by both technical and non-technical teams.
notable: Launched monday CRM, monday dev, monday service, and monday campaigns.
Introduced monday agents, an AI agent builder.
Q3 2025 revenue of $316.9M (+26% YoY), projected $1.2B for full year 2025.

competitors.name: Notion
competitors.tagline: An all-in-one workspace for note-taking, project management, knowledge sharing, and collaboration.
competitors.subCategory: Prioritizes documentation and knowledge management with a flexible, block-based database system.
notable: Launched Notion Calendar and Notion Mail.
Crossed $500M in annual revenue by 2025.
$270M employee share sale at $11B valuation in early 2026.

competitors.name: YouTrack
competitors.tagline: A project management and issue tracking tool for agile teams.
competitors.subCategory: Deeply integrated with JetBrains' developer tools and IDEs.
notable: Launched remote MCP server and new AI-powered apps in October 2025.
Introduced Whiteboards for collaborative planning in 2026.
New vertical side panel navigation and dynamic natural language filters.

competitors.name: Plane
competitors.tagline: An open-source project management tool for software development.
competitors.subCategory: Offers an open-source, self-hostable path with added flexibility like timeline/Gantt views.
notable: Open-source, self-hostable alternative to Linear and Jira.

competitors.name: Bugasura
competitors.tagline: A bug-reporting-first platform for QA-heavy engineering teams.
competitors.subCategory: Optimizes the front end of the bug lifecycle with high-fidelity capture and AI-assisted triage.
notable: Launched Unified Reports Dashboard for multiple projects.
AI-generated issue descriptions and automatic suggestions.

competitors.name: Shortcut
competitors.tagline: A project management platform built specifically for software development teams.
competitors.subCategory: Balances the simplicity of Linear with more robust reporting and planning features.
notable: Direct Linear alternative targeting software dev teams.

competitors.name: Height
competitors.tagline: A project management tool that focuses on task automation and workflow efficiency.
competitors.subCategory: Uses a spreadsheet-like interface for task management that allows for complex data manipulation.
notable: Spreadsheet-like task management with complex data manipulation.

competitors.name: Zenhub
competitors.tagline: A productivity management platform that integrates directly into GitHub.
competitors.subCategory: GitHub-native approach that keeps developers within their standard coding workflow.
notable: AI label suggestions and AI Early Access program.
Zenhub Enterprise 4.3.0 with enhanced roadmap functionality.

competitors.name: OpenProject
competitors.tagline: An open-source project management software for classic, agile, or hybrid approaches.
competitors.subCategory: Provides a self-hosted, open-source solution with strong data privacy and governance.
notable: 28 releases in 2024 with features like GitLab integration.
OpenProject Mobile app launched in Beta.

competitors.name: Redmine
competitors.tagline: A flexible web-based project management web application.
competitors.subCategory: A long-standing, free, and open-source tool that is highly customizable for various project types.
notable: Released Redmine 6.0 in November 2024 with 146 changes and fixes.

competitors.name: Productboard
competitors.tagline: A product management platform that bridges customer input and development.
competitors.subCategory: Focuses on product prioritization and customer feedback management rather than just issue tracking.
notable: Raised $125M Series D at $1.725B valuation.
Raised $72M Series C to accelerate enterprise growth.

competitors.name: Trello
competitors.tagline: A visual-first project management tool using Kanban boards.
competitors.subCategory: Prioritizes a simple, visual-first approach that is accessible to both technical and non-technical users.
notable: Launched Mirror cards to sync tasks across multiple boards.
AI-powered Quick Capture from Slack, Teams, and email.
Trello Planner for time-blocking tasks in Google Calendar.
